name: Muscadine grape (food medium)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Legacy SR whole-berry panel.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Individual sugar assay, whole berry; one of the two co-dominant carbon sources. Muscadine juice means (Ector, Optimum quality parameters of muscadine grape juices) give glucose avg 5.16% (range 3.52-7.70%) on a juice basis.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Co-dominant carbon source; fructose > glucose, as in most grapes. Juice means (Ector) fructose avg 5.51% (3.35-9.28%).

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor sugar; muscadine juice sucrose is variable (Ector avg 1.89%, 0-5.2%). Whole-berry USDA value used.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Sandhu & Gu 2013 / Ector, muscadine 58-variety survey (malate avg 0.50%, range 0.17-1.16%)
components.evidence: Malate + tartrate constitute ~90% of muscadine organic acids at ripeness (Antioxidants 2021, 10(6):914, doi:10.3390/antiox10060914). Not reported by USDA; a dominant organic carbon/energy source. 'Roanoke' cultivar reaches 0.70%.

components.name: tartaric acid
components.source: muscadine 58-variety survey (tartrate avg 0.26%, range 0.15-0.52%); Foods 2021,10(5):1101 (2.6-4.8 g/L juice)
components.evidence: Second dominant organic acid; becomes proportionally higher as berries ripen. Not reported by USDA.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Present only in trace quantities in muscadine (with pyruvic, fumaric, succinic, quinic); unquantified whole-berry value.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Low total protein; supplies amino acids / assimilable nitrogen.

components.name: free amino acids (alanine, histidine, arginine predominant; also valine, glutamine, aspartate, proline, serine, threonine)
components.evidence: Nine amino acids (alanine, histidine, arginine, valine, glutamine, aspartic acid, proline, serine, threonine) = ~90% of pulp free-AA pool; alanine/histidine/arginine principal in juice. Arginine and proline rise with ripening (assimilable-N sources). Quantitative per-100g values not reported.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Whole berry incl. seed lipid; muscadine is not a fatty food, fatty-acid breakdown not resolved in USDA legacy record.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: High for a grape (thick skin + seeds); largely non-fermentable structural carbohydrate.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: Phosphate source.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Single consolidated iron value (replaces the model-compartment duplicates 'Iron (Fe3+)' and 'Fe2+ mitochondria').

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: Genuinely high; muscadine (Vitis rotundifolia) is a notably Mn-rich fruit. Real, not noise.

components.name: riboflavin (vitamin B2)
components.evidence: USDA legacy record for muscadine stores riboflavin = 1.500 mg/100g, which is biologically implausible (comparable grapes ~0.07 mg/100g) and is a documented USDA data-entry outlier. Reported here at the grape-family reference level (~0.07-0.09 mg); LOW confidence on the exact figure.

components.name: folate (vitamin B9)
components.evidence: Total folate.

components.name: vitamin A
components.evidence: = 67 IU; derived from provitamin-A carotenoids, NOT preformed retinol (see remove[]).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Provitamin-A carotenoid; the true source of the vitamin-A activity.

components.name: alpha-carotene
components.evidence: Trace carotenoid.

components.name: beta-cryptoxanthin
components.evidence: Trace carotenoid.

components.name: lutein + zeaxanthin
components.evidence: Xanthophyll carotenoids (non-provitamin-A).

components.name: ammonium
components.evidence: Genuine inorganic nitrogen source in grape must (part of yeast-assimilable nitrogen); retained but unquantified for muscadine.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed retinol is an animal-derived vitamer; plants/grapes contain provitamin-A carotenoids (beta-carotene) not retinol. FooDB artifact; vitamin-A activity is captured by the carotenoids instead.

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: Metabolic-model exchange/gas species, not a food-composition nutrient.

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: Metabolic-model exchange/gas species, not a food nutrient.

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: Proton/pH model artifact, not a quantifiable food nutrient.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Model compartment-tagged duplicate of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Redox/species-tagged duplicate of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt: not measured in the USDA muscadine record and not a defining nutrient; generic FooDB trace-mineral placeholder.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace element, not a measured muscadine nutrient; FooDB generic-mineral padding.

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: Molybdenum trace element, unmeasured in muscadine; generic FooDB placeholder, not growth-defining.

missing_added: quantitative individual sugar amounts (glucose 3.67, fructose 3.92, sucrose 0.57 g/100g)
total protein + free amino acid profile (alanine/histidine/arginine)
quantitative minerals (K, Ca, Mg, P, Na, Fe, Zn, Cu, Mn)
keep_uncovered: ellagic acid (signature muscadine phenolic, exceptionally high vs other grapes; no BiGG met)
resveratrol / trans-resveratrol (stilbene, characteristic of muscadine skin)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 173040 (Grapes, muscadine, raw) - full whole-berry nutrient panel
Ector et al., Optimum quality parameters of muscadine grape juices/beverages (sugar & acid means: fructose 5.51%, glucose 5.16%, sucrose 1.89%; malate 0.50%, tartrate 0.26%)
Sandhu & Gu, Fruit Quality/Nutraceutical properties of 58 muscadine varieties (Vitis rotundifolia)
Antioxidants 2021, 10(6):914, doi:10.3390/antiox10060914 (organic-acid profile; malate+tartrate ~90% at ripeness)
Lamikanra O. 1997, free amino acids of Noble muscadine, PMID 10606539
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are D-glucose and D-fructose (~3.7-3.9 g/100g each) plus the previously-missing organic acids malic (~0.50 g/100g) and tartaric (~0.26 g/100g); sucrose is minor. Muscadine is distinctively high in manganese, dietary fiber, and skin phenolics (ellagic acid, resveratrol). Retinol was removed as an implausible animal vitamer (carotenoids substituted); the USDA riboflavin figure (1.5 mg) is a known implausible outlier and was down-corrected to the grape-family reference level with low confidence.
